question_text: The distribution of (X3 −µ)TS−1(X3 −µ) is χ2 6 (Chi-square distribution with 6 degrees of freedom). Then which of the above statements is/are true?
solution:
Step 1: Analyzing Statement (I).
The Wishart distribution Wp(n, Σ) is the distribution of the sample covariance matrix S for a
random sample from a multivariate normal distribution. Since X1, X2, . . . , X20 are from a
N6(µ, Σ) distribution, the distribution of 19S will indeed be W6(19, Σ), where the order is 6
and the degrees of freedom are 19. This makes statement (I) true.
Step 2: Analyzing Statement (II).
The quantity (X3 −µ)TS−1(X3 −µ) follows a χ2 distribution with degrees of freedom equal
to the number of variables, which is 6 in this case. Therefore, statement (II) is also true.
Step 3: Conclusion.
Both statements (I) and (II) are true, so the correct answer is (C).
Quick Tip
In a multivariate normal distribution, the sample covariance matrix S follows a Wishart
distribution. For a normal sample, linear combinations like (X −µ)TS−1(X −µ) follow
a Chi-square distribution with degrees of freedom equal to the dimension of the data.
